matched.reason: Both promises refer to Errtu's pursuit of the Crystal Shard Crenshinibon, introduced in the prelude.
Both describe Crenshinibon manipulating its wielder Kessell for destructive conquest, starting at chapter 4.
Both address Regis's dangerous past as a fugitive from the thieves' guild of Calimport, introduced in chapter 2.
Both promise a united barbarian horde attacking Ten-Towns, opened in chapter 2.
Both are about Bruenor's goal to reclaim the lost dwarven kingdom of Mithril Hall, opened at chapter 5.
Both describe Bruenor forging a legendary warhammer, Aegis-fang, in chapter 11.
Both involve Wulfgar receiving the magical hammer Aegis-fang, opened in chapter 12.
Both set up Drizzt training Wulfgar as his mentor, opened in chapter 12.
Both depict Errtu entering the material plane to seek Crenshinibon, opening in chapter 10.
Both reveal Errtu's plan to betray Kessell and take the crystal shard, opened in chapter 13.
Both promise Drizzt summoning Errtu by true name to force information, in chapter 25.
Both state Drizzt obtains a magical ice scimitar from the dragon's hoard, opened in chapter 22.
Both describe Errtu's century-long revenge vow after banishment, in chapter 25.
Both refer to the companions' quest to find Mithril Hall, opened in chapter 30.
Both introduce the assassin Artemis Entreri hunting Regis on Pasha Pook's orders, in the epilogue.

unmatchedGold.promise_text: Crenshinibon is a sought-after relic of great power, coveted by the demon Errtu.
Regis is a fugitive from the thieves' guild of Calimport, and his stolen ruby pendant has magical charm powers.
A united barbarian horde is gathering to launch a massive attack on Ten-Towns.
Ten-Towns is fractured by internal rivalries, making a unified defense against external threats difficult.
Crenshinibon is an ancient sentient artifact of evil that manipulates its wielder and hungers for conquest.
Bruenor yearns to reclaim Mithril Hall, his lost ancestral dwarven kingdom.
The barbarian king Heafstaag survives the Battle of Bryn Shander, carried away by loyal warriors.
Wulfgar, a barbarian indentured to Bruenor, nears the end of his service, harboring a blood-debt and great potential.
Akar Kessell is assembling a vast multi-tribe monster army to conquer Icewind Dale.
The demon Errtu enters the material plane seeking Crenshinibon and becomes Kessell's general.
Bruenor is guided by a prophetic dream to forge a legendary warhammer.
Bruenor asks Drizzt to train Wulfgar, setting up a mentor-student relationship.
Wulfgar receives the magical hammer Aegis-fang and swears a conditional oath not to raise it against Ten-Towns.
Errtu secretly plots to betray Kessell and claim the crystal shard for itself.
Heafstaag is magically coerced into serving Kessell, fostering deep resentment.
The Council of Ten-Towns fails to unite against Kessell's second invasion, leaving the region vulnerable.
Wulfgar departs on a secret personal quest, implied to involve a dragon.
Drizzt obtains an extraordinary magical ice scimitar from the dragon Icingdeath's hoard.
The dwarves of Mithril Hall survive the initial clash and will fight again.
Drizzt summons Errtu using its true name to force it to reveal Cryshal-Tirith's weakness.
Errtu vows a century-long revenge after Drizzt banishes it back to the Abyss.
Wulfgar, now king, rallies the barbarian tribes to fight against Kessell's army.
The dwarves prepare a surprise tunnel assault from beneath the enemy encampment.
Cassius sends Regis as an emissary to use the ruby pendant on Kessell.
Regis tricks Kessell into believing there are traitors in his ranks, aiming to sow chaos.
The companions vow to undertake a quest to find the lost dwarven kingdom of Mithril Hall.
The assassin Artemis Entreri, sent by Pasha Pook, is hunting Regis.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Errtu will continue to seek and pursue the Crystal Shard Crenshinibon.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Errtu sits on its mushroom throne in the Abyss, impatiently awaiting news of the relic Crenshinibon.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Acid drool slipped freely from the demon's mouth when it saw the tower image of the relic.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: The Crystal Shard Crenshinibon will be found and used.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Crenshinibon comes to rest in the northern mountains of the Forgotten Realms, settling into snow and waiting.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Then he felt . . . warmth. ... He dug his hand into the warm area and pulled free a crystal shard.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Some wizards in the caravan have darker, hidden purposes.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: The caravan's destination is Ten-Towns under Kelvin's Cairn, sought for its scrimshaw trade — though some wizards have darker purposes.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "How fortunate to find so convenient a tool," Dendybar noted.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Kessell will be betrayed and discarded by the conspirators who used him.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Dendybar signals Eldeluc to be rid of Kessell; Eldeluc sends him to the stables to announce the night departure, and Kessell asks what to do with the body.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: He understood now that he had been no more than a pawn in their devious designs to rid themselves of Morkai the Red.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Crenshinibon will manipulate and control Kessell, using him for its own destructive purposes.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: The narrator reveals the true nature of Crenshinibon — an ancient sentient relic of darkest evil that draws power from daylight and is already scheming to control its weak-willed new host.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Kessell was engulfed by the avalanche, rejected by Crenshinibon and buried beneath the mountain.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Kessell will build an army and conquer Ten-Towns using the shard's power.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: That night Crenshinibon seeds Kessell's dreams with visions of goblin servitude, and Kessell wakes liking the idea and then discovers a scrying room in the tower.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Goblins and orcs fell to their knees, and all the vast army took up the chant of 'Kessell! Kessell!' paying homage to the wizard with a fanatical devotion

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Regis's past with the thieves' guild and Pasha Pook will catch up to him.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Regis reflects on being uniquely far north for a halfling — held here by trouble with a thieves-guild guildmaster — and an unfinished ivory carving embodies his procrastination.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: the emerald-hilted dagger... the mark of Artemis Entreri, Pasha Pook's prime assassin.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: A united barbarian horde will invade and attack Ten-Towns.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt presses on despite sunlight sickness because ominous signs — empty barbarian caves, abandoned migration trails, and war drums — warn of imminent attack on Ten-Towns.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: The horde entered the mouth of Bremen's Run just before midday.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Regis's ruby pendant possesses magical persuasive powers.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: But it is there - I see it clearly and know the legend of such a stone.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Ten-seconds passed and Kemp had not blinked.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Bruenor will seek to reclaim the lost dwarven homeland of Mithril Hall.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Bruenor's thoughts drift to Mithril Hall, his lost ancestral home of silver rivers and dwarven smiths, and to the clan's exile and dwindling numbers.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "Off to Mithril Hall, then!" Bruenor roared.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Bruenor will forge a powerful enchanted warhammer, Aegis-fang.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Bruenor recognizes the dream-vision of a two-headed hammer and understands he must act quickly before the solstice full moon.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: The hammer had captured every speck of the diamond dust.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: The warhammer Aegis-fang will be given to Wulfgar.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt teases Bruenor about gifting the warhammer to Wulfgar while genuinely admiring its craftsmanship.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "I'll place no bonds on yer will, but I'll have yer oath, for me own good conscience, that ye'll never raise this weapon against the people o' Ten-Tbwns!"

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Wulfgar has a secret, dangerous vow to fulfill — to slay the dragon Icingdeath.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar cryptically invokes an unfulfilled vow of heritage, and Catti-brie immediately sees through his posturing.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Drizzt bore witness to their victory. The great icicle fell onto the dragon, snapping its neck.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Drizzt will train Wulfgar in combat.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Bruenor admits he needs Drizzt to train Wulfgar before winter, revealing the depth of his unspoken paternal bond.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: At night, they fought.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Errtu will answer the summoning and come to the material plane to seek Crenshinibon.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: On the Abyss, Errtu hears the weak summoning call and chooses to answer not out of compulsion but because it has been waiting for years for a path to the material plane.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: Now the grotesque, monkeylike head, with the maw and muzzle of a dog and the oversized incisors of a boar, was visible, the huge, blood-red eyes squinting from within the brazier's flame.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Errtu will eventually betray Kessell and try to take the shard.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Errtu privately judges Kessell unfit to lead and reveals it secretly offered the shard a deal to replace him, which Crenshinibon refused because it prefers a weak master.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "The tower's walls reflect any attacks, but the crystal heart is its only vulnerability."

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Wulfgar will challenge and confront Heafstaag for leadership of the barbarian tribe.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Wulfgar is shamed to learn his own tribe follows the one-eyed Heafstaag; Drizzt orders a warning sent to Bryn Shander.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "I am Wulfgar, son of Beornegar... I have come to challenge Heafstaag for leadership of the Tribe of the Elk!"

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Drizzt will summon Errtu using his true name to confront him.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt recognizes not just the demon type but the specific creature—Errtu, a demon he knew from Menzoberranzan—and realizes he knows its true name and can summon it.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: And so Errtu was quite receptive, even relieved, when it heard Drizzt's call floating down the valley.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Drizzt's scimitar from the dragon's hoard possesses magical icy properties.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt waves off treasure except for a few baubles, then uncovers a gem-encrusted scimitar of extraordinary craftsmanship and claims it.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: the drow's dragon-hoard scimitar bit into Errtu's knee, and the icy magic of the blade quenched the demon fire and wounded the demon grievously.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: Errtu vows a century-long revenge after being banished by Drizzt.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Errtu is banished, issuing its century-long vow of revenge as it dissolves into steam and vapor while the scimitar clangs to the stone.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "I shall return for you, Drizzt Do'Urden! A century shall pass before you see the last of Errtu!"

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: The companions will embark on a quest to find Mithril Hall.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Drizzt promises to accompany Bruenor to Mithril Hall in the spring, and the dying dwarf smiles with contentment.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: "Off to Mithril Hall, then!" Bruenor roared.

unmatchedPredicted.promise_text: The assassin Artemis Entreri is hunting Regis, sent by Pasha Pook.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_open: Luck saves Regis when he spots the stranger first and recognizes the emerald-hilted dagger of Artemis Entreri, Pasha Pook's prime assassin.
unmatchedPredicted.evidence_quote_close: the emerald-hilted dagger... the mark of Artemis Entreri, Pasha Pook's prime assassin.
